title: Workplace Sickness: How Countries Are Trying to Boost Their Economies
subtitle: Governments in the UK and Germany are looking at how to manage sick leave to improve national productivity.

summary.en: Many nations are currently looking for ways to improve their economic growth by changing how they handle workplace sickness. In the UK, a new taskforce aims to help people with long-term health issues return to their jobs. Meanwhile, Germany is introducing stricter rules for getting sick notes to reduce the number of days employees spend away from work. Business leaders argue that these changes could unlock hidden economic potential. However, critics warn that these new policies might unfairly pressure workers who are truly ill.
summary.zh: 許多國家目前正尋求透過改變職場病假處理方式來提升經濟成長。在英國，一個新的專案小組旨在協助有長期健康問題的民眾重返職場。與此同時，德國正引進更嚴格的病假證明取得規定，以減少員工缺勤天數。企業領袖主張這些變革能釋放隱藏的經濟潛力。然而，批評者警告，這些新政策可能會對真正生病的勞工造成不公平的壓力。

paragraphs.en: Across Europe, governments are searching for new ways to strengthen their economies. A major part of this effort involves looking at how employees use sick leave. In both the United Kingdom and Germany, leaders are debating how to balance the health needs of workers with the need for a productive, active workforce. These discussions are happening as both countries face economic challenges and look for ways to boost growth without needing to build new houses or change immigration rules.
paragraphs.zh: 歐洲各國政府正尋求新途徑來強化經濟。這項努力的核心在於檢視員工如何使用病假。在英國與德國，領導階層正針對如何平衡勞工健康需求與維持高生產力、活躍勞動力之間進行辯論。這些討論發生的背景，是兩國皆面臨經濟挑戰，並試圖在不需興建新屋或改變移民法規的情況下，尋找促進成長的方法。

paragraphs.en: In the United Kingdom, a new group called the Get Britain Working taskforce has been formed to address the issue of long-term illness. According to BBC Business, more than 250 of the country’s largest employers—including major names like Tesco, British Airways, and Royal Mail—have joined this initiative. The goal is to prevent people from leaving the workforce permanently due to health problems and to help those who are currently away to return to their jobs. Sir Charlie Mayfield, the former chair of John Lewis, leads this group. He explained to BBC Business that there is a large amount of economic growth currently "hiding in plain sight" if companies can better support their staff.
paragraphs.zh: 在英國，一個名為「讓英國動起來」（Get Britain Working）的專案小組已經成立，旨在解決長期病假的問題。根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，超過兩百五十家英國大型雇主，包括特易購（Tesco）、英國航空（British Airways）與皇家郵政（Royal Mail）等知名企業，皆已加入這項倡議。其目標是防止人們因健康問題永久退出職場，並協助目前休假中的員工重返工作崗位。曾任約翰路易斯百貨（John Lewis）董事長的查理·梅菲爾德爵士（Sir Charlie Mayfield）領導該小組。他向BBC商業新聞解釋，若企業能更妥善地支援員工，將能釋放出大量「顯而易見卻被忽視」的經濟成長潛力。

paragraphs.en: Sir Charlie noted that many people who are signed off work for months often lose all contact with their employers. He argued that this is not because employers are unkind, but because communication often breaks down when someone is ill. By tracking sickness absence and return-to-work outcomes, the taskforce hopes to make workplace health performance clearer. The hope is that by fixing these problems, the government can reduce the national welfare bill, which is expected to take up a large portion of government spending in the coming years. Sir Charlie believes that this is a "win-win" situation for employers, employees, and taxpayers.
paragraphs.zh: 梅菲爾德爵士指出，許多請長假數月的員工往往與雇主失去聯繫。他認為這並非雇主不近人情，而是因為當員工生病時，溝通管道經常中斷。透過追蹤病假缺勤與復職情況，該專案小組希望讓職場健康表現更透明。各界期望透過解決這些問題，政府能降低國家福利支出，因為這項支出預計在未來幾年將佔政府預算極大比例。梅菲爾德爵士認為，這對雇主、員工與納稅人而言是「三贏」局面。

paragraphs.en: However, the situation in Germany is slightly different. According to Deutsche Welle (DW), the German government is taking a more direct approach to rising absence rates. Chancellor Friedrich Merz recently announced a plan to crack down on the high number of sick days taken by German workers. Research from the IGES Institute shows that the average number of sick days per year has risen from about 13 days in 2018 to 19.5 days today. To address this, the government plans to stop the practice of getting a sick note over the phone. Starting next year, workers will be required to visit a doctor in person on the very first day of their illness.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，德國的情況則略有不同。根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle, DW）報導，德國政府正採取更直接的方式來應對日益攀升的缺勤率。總理梅爾茲（Friedrich Merz）近期宣布一項計畫，旨在打擊德國勞工請病假天數過高的現象。德國IGES研究所（IGES Institute）的研究顯示，每人每年平均病假天數已從二〇一八年的約十三天，上升至今日的十九點五天。為了解決此問題，政府計畫終止透過電話取得病假證明的方式。自明年起，勞工在生病的第一天起，就必須親自前往診所看診。

paragraphs.en: Germany has historically had one of the most generous sick leave systems in the world. Workers can receive 100% of their salary for up to six weeks of leave, which is paid by their employer. After that, health insurance covers a portion of their pay for a much longer period. Supporters of the new rules, including Chancellor Merz, argue that the current high level of absence is a "competitive disadvantage" that hurts the economy. They believe the changes will restore fairness and help businesses manage their staff more effectively. However, the German government is also facing criticism. Some opponents argue that these new rules could stigmatize people who are genuinely sick and unfairly blame the workforce for broader economic problems like high energy costs and global competition.
paragraphs.zh: 德國向來擁有全球最優渥的病假制度之一。勞工可領取全額薪資長達六週，且由雇主支付。此後，健康保險將在更長的一段時間內支付部分薪資。包括梅爾茲總理在內的新規支持者主張，目前的高缺勤率是一種損害經濟的「競爭劣勢」。他們認為這些變革將恢復公平性，並協助企業更有效地管理員工。然而，德國政府也面臨批評。部分反對者認為，這些新規定可能會讓真正生病的人蒙受汙名，並將能源成本高昂與全球競爭等更廣泛的經濟問題，不公平地歸咎於勞工身上。

paragraphs.en: There is also a debate about why these numbers are rising. According to the IGES Institute report cited by DW, part of the increase in Germany is due to better reporting. A new electronic system for sick notes, introduced in 2023, makes it much easier to track absences that were previously recorded on paper. Additionally, the COVID-19 pandemic changed how people think about health; workers are now much more likely to stay home when they have a cold to avoid spreading germs to their colleagues.
paragraphs.zh: 關於這些數據為何上升，各界也存在爭論。根據德國之聲引述的IGES研究所報告，德國缺勤率上升的部分原因在於通報機制改善。二〇二三年引進的電子病假證明系統，讓追蹤過去以紙本記錄的缺勤變得容易許多。此外，新冠肺炎（COVID-19）疫情改變了人們對健康的看法；勞工現在更有可能在感冒時選擇留在家中，以避免將病菌傳染給同事。

paragraphs.en: As these countries move forward, the debate highlights a difficult challenge. On one hand, businesses need a consistent workforce to stay competitive in a difficult global market. On the other hand, employees need to feel supported when they are unwell. While some employers in the UK have expressed concerns that they cannot afford to invest in new health programs due to rising taxes, others remain hopeful. The outcome of these policies will be watched closely by other nations, as every government tries to find the right balance between a healthy, protected workforce and a strong, growing economy.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著這些國家持續推動政策，這場辯論凸顯了一項艱鉅的挑戰。一方面，企業需要穩定的勞動力以在艱困的全球市場中保持競爭力；另一方面，員工在身體不適時需要感受到支持。儘管英國部分雇主表示，由於稅收增加，他們無力投資新的健康計畫，但其他人仍抱持希望。這些政策的成效將受到其他國家的密切關注，因為各國政府都在嘗試在健康、受保障的勞動力與強勁成長的經濟之間，找到正確的平衡點。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Existing or being present in an obvious place, but remaining unnoticed.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 就在眼前卻沒被發現；顯而易見卻被忽略。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個慣用語，字面意思容易誤導，指事物其實很明顯，但因為太過平常而沒被注意到。文中：He explained to BBC Business that there is a large amount of economic growth currently "hiding in plain sight" if companies can better support their staff.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be officially authorized by a doctor to stay away from work due to illness.
keyTerms.noteZh: 在職場語境中，這不是指「簽名結束」，而是指醫生開立證明讓員工休假。文中：Sir Charlie noted that many people who are signed off work for months often lose all contact with their employers.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To start dealing with bad or illegal behavior in a more severe way.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的動詞片語，用來形容政府或權威機構採取強硬手段解決問題。文中：Chancellor Friedrich Merz recently announced a plan to crack down on the high number of sick days taken by German workers.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Tackle workplace sickness to unlock hidden growth, former John Lewis boss says

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe

quiz.question: According to the article, what specific change is the German government implementing to address rising sick leave rates?

options.A: Reducing the salary percentage paid to employees during their first six weeks of illness.
options.B: Requiring employees to visit a doctor in person on the first day of their sickness.
options.C: Limiting the total number of sick days an employee can take per year to 13 days.
options.D: Transferring the responsibility of sick pay from employers to the national health insurance system.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，德國政府計畫取消電話請假制度，並要求員工從生病的第一天起就必須親自去看醫生。選項 A 錯誤，文中並未提及降低薪資給付比例；選項 C 錯誤，13 天是 2018 年的平均請假天數，而非限制天數；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到雇主仍需負擔前六週的薪資，並未改變此結構。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the reason for the increase in recorded sick days in Germany?

options.A: German workers have become significantly less healthy since the COVID-19 pandemic.
options.B: Employers are intentionally reporting false absences to lower their tax burdens.
options.C: Technological and behavioral changes have made absences more visible than they were in the past.
options.D: The German workforce is intentionally taking more sick days to protest against high energy costs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，請假數據上升的部分原因是「更好的報告系統」（電子化系統讓紀錄更精確）以及疫情後人們更傾向於生病時留在家中。選項 A 無法由文中證實，僅提到人們對健康的態度改變；選項 B 與文中事實相反；選項 D 則屬於過度推論，文中並未指控員工有抗議行為。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, what does Sir Charlie Mayfield mean when he says economic growth is 'hiding in plain sight'?

options.A: The growth is obvious but is currently being ignored or overlooked.
options.B: The growth is being hidden by companies to avoid paying higher taxes.
options.C: The growth is difficult to find because it is located in secret government files.
options.D: The growth is physically invisible and requires special technology to measure.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。「Hiding in plain sight」是一個慣用語，意指某事物就在眼前，但因為人們沒有注意或未採取行動而顯得隱形。文中指如果企業能更好地支持員工，這些潛在的生產力與成長其實一直都在，只是未被開發。選項 B、C、D 皆曲解了此慣用語在商業脈絡下的含義。

quiz.question: What is the central theme of the article regarding sick leave policies in Europe?

options.A: The United Kingdom and Germany are collaborating to create a unified European sick leave policy.
options.B: Governments are struggling to balance the need for economic productivity with the welfare of their workers.
options.C: Strict regulations on sick leave are the only effective way to boost national economic growth.
options.D: Employees are increasingly abusing sick leave systems, forcing governments to take drastic measures.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。全文的核心在於探討英國與德國如何面對勞動力健康與經濟成長之間的兩難（Trade-off），兩國皆試圖在支持員工需求與維持競爭力之間尋求平衡。選項 A 錯誤，兩國是各自採取行動；選項 C 過於絕對，文中也提到反對意見；選項 D 僅反映了部分觀點，並非文章的主旨。
